description_long: This is a wooden picket fence section composed of vertical planks with pointed tops, evenly spaced and attached to two horizontal rails. The wood grain is visible, giving it a natural, rustic appearance suitable for suburban yards, parks, or gardens. The front side shows the vertical pickets without visible rails, while the back side reveals two horizontal support rails running across the width. The fence is relatively short in height, making it more decorative or boundary-marking rather than a security barrier. The construction appears lightweight, likely allowing for manual repositioning. There are no visible decorative elements or post caps, and the design is simple and functional.
description: A short wooden picket fence with pointed tops and two horizontal rails, featuring a natural wood grain finish. The design is simple and functional, suitable for marking boundaries in urban or suburban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing evenly spaced vertical pickets with pointed tops and no visible rails.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence, with the pickets and rails aligned.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the two horizontal rails supporting the vertical pickets.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile of the fence.
